mechanism_narrative: A rising Industrial Production index indicates expanding manufacturing output, which translates into stronger corporate earnings and higher equity valuations over a quarterly horizon. A sustained increase in INDPRO leads to positive S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between INDPRO month-over-month changes and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is non-positive, or the Rank IC over any 3-year window is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, it signals labor market slack that depresses consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative short-term equity returns. A crossing above this threshold triggers a negative signal for the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods when UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, 5-day forward SPX returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a stable or increasing federal funds rate imply tightening real monetary conditions. This interaction depresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 3% and FEDFUNDS stable or rising over 3 months
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint signal over 3-year rolling windows is not significantly negative, or triggered instances yield positive returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and retail sales over two consecutive months indicate peaking aggregate demand in the late-cycle expansion phase. This demand weakness signals negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: In periods when both series decline, the average 20-day forward return is not negative, or the signal's Sharpe ratio over the full sample is below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls signal a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer income and spending, driving corporate earnings and equity prices higher. This trend effect is always active and predicts positive S&P 500 20-day returns.
failure_condition: The correlation between PAYEMS monthly changes and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is non-positive over any 5-year window, or the Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate above 0.5% indicates accelerating economic expansion, which improves corporate earnings prospects and lifts equity valuations over the subsequent quarter. This threshold-triggered signal is directionally positive.
failure_condition: When the trigger is active, the average 60-day forward SPX return is not positive, or the signal fails to outperform the unconditional mean by at least 100 bps annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production over three months, combined with a high federal funds rate above 4%, creates a stagflationary environment. Reduced output and tight monetary policy jointly depress equity prices, leading to negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declining over 3 months and FEDFUNDS > 4%
failure_condition: In triggered periods, the 20-day forward return is not negative on average, or the proportion of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls combined with a rise in the unemployment rate signals imminent labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings. This interaction triggers negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS decreases month-over-month and UNRATE increases
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the average 20-day forward return is not negative, or the hit rate (negative returns) is below 60%.
